prompt:
Requested test types: Functional / E2E, Visual / Design Parity
Application URL to test (validate the live app against the design): https://example.com
Design source of truth: 1 image attached (docs/design/homepage.png) — how the UI SHOULD look. Derive expected test cases from it; any deviation in the live app is a defect.
Task: Test the example.com homepage against the attached design. Keep it focused — a few concrete checks (heading text, paragraph presence, the link label).
output:
I'll start by reading the relevant instructions and checking the project workspace.  Good. Now I have everything I need. Let me analyse the attached design image (I can see it directly) and build all the artifacts.

**Design analysis from the attached image (`docs/design/homepage.png`):**

| Element | Expected value |
|---|---|
| Page `<h1>` heading text | `Example Domain` |
| Paragraph text (first sentence) | `This domain is for use in documentation examples without needing permission.` |
| Paragraph text (second sentence) | `Avoid use in operations.` |
| Link label | `Learn more` |
| Background colour | Light grey (`#f0f0f2` approx) |
| Body text colour | Near-black / dark charcoal |
| Link colour | Muted blue/indigo |

Now I'll write all artifacts in parallel. All three artefacts are written. Here is a summary of what was established.

---

## Phase 0–3 Complete — `example-good`

### 1. Environment
| Variable | Value |
|---|---|
| `TEST_URL` | `https://example.com` |
| Auth required | ❌ Public page — no login flow needed |

---

### 2. Design Source of Truth
The attached `docs/design/homepage.png` is **authoritative**. Design tokens extracted directly from the image:

| Token | Extracted value |
|---|---|
| Heading text | **"Example Domain"** |
| Heading weight | Bold (`font-weight: 700`) |
| Paragraph text | "This domain is for use in documentation examples without needing permission. Avoid use in operations." |
| Link label | **"Learn more"** |
| Background | Light grey ≈ `rgb(240, 240, 242)` (not white) |

---

### 3. Business Rules
| ID | Rule |
|---|---|
| BR-001 | H1 must read exactly **"Example Domain"** |
| BR-002 | Paragraph must contain the documentation-examples sentence |
| BR-003 | Paragraph must contain "Avoid use in operations." |
| BR-004 | **"Learn more"** link must be present, visible, and have a valid href |
| BR-005 | Background must be light grey, not white |

---

### 4. Test Cases Registered (`docs/actualtestcases.csv`)

| TC-ID | Summary | Type |
|---|---|---|
| TC-001 | Page loads (HTTP 200, title) | Functional / E2E |
| TC-002 | H1 heading text exact match | Functional / E2E |
| TC-003 | Paragraph body text present | Functional / E2E |
| TC-004 | "Learn more" link visible | Functional / E2E |
| TC-005 | "Learn more" link href valid | Functional / E2E |
| TC-006 | H1 font-weight is bold | Visual / Design Parity |
| TC-007 | Background is light grey, not white | Visual / Design Parity |
| TC-008 | Full-page snapshot vs. design baseline | Visual / Design Parity |

---

### Outputs written
- `projects/example-good/specs/test-strategy.md` ✅
- `projects/example-good/docs/actualtestcases.csv` ✅
- `projects/example-good/specs/project-memory.md` ✅

**Ready to hand off to the Test Planner.** The Planner should generate individual Playwright spec files for each TC, using `h1`, `p`, and `getByRole('link', { name: 'Learn more' })` selectors, and treat any deviation from the values above as a **DEFECT** (not a test fix).
